pairs.question: What NFL team did Charles Edward Brown play for in 1979?
pairs.answer: Charles Edward Brown played for the St. Louis Cardinals in 1979. He was added to their roster in November 1979 and played in 4 of the team's last 5 games that season.

pairs.question: How many games did Charles Edward Brown play in the NFL, and for which teams?
pairs.answer: Charles Edward Brown played a total of 4 NFL games in 1979, all for the St. Louis Cardinals.

pairs.question: What position did Charles Edward Brown play in high school and college?
pairs.answer: In high school, Brown played as a defensive lineman at Dulles High School. In college, he played as the starting center for the University of Houston Cougars from 1976 to 1978.

pairs.question: What honors did Charles Edward Brown receive during his college football career?
pairs.answer: During his college football career at the University of Houston, Brown was named to the Associated Press All-Southwest Conference team in 1978 and was also named the Southwest Conference Lineman of the Year that same season.

pairs.question: Why did Charles Edward Brown believe he went undrafted out of college?
pairs.answer: Brown believed he went undrafted due to his height, which was just about 6 feet tall.

pairs.question: What was the career path of Charles Edward Brown after being cut by the Houston Oilers in 1979?
pairs.answer: After being cut by the Houston Oilers following one preseason game in 1979, Brown was signed by the St. Louis Cardinals in November 1979. He played 4 games for the Cardinals before being cut again during the 1980 preseason.

pairs.question: Who was Charles Edward Brown's father, and did he play professional football?
pairs.answer: Charles Edward Brown's father was also named Charlie Brown. He played for the University of Houston Cougars and signed a contract with the Oakland Raiders, though it is not specified if he played in any NFL games.
